summary:
Kraissl’s lines denote optimal sites for minimal-tension incisions due to their alignment with natural relaxation lines; they help reduce post-surgical scar formation by minimizing tissue stress during healing. To achieve better cosmetic outcomes, surgeons aim to align cuts along these naturally less tense paths identified as Kraissl’s lines. ```plaintext
Kraissl's lines signify regions where there is least skin tension, facilitating incision placement to minimize scars. These lines guide surgeons towards creating more aesthetically pleasing results through lower tension at wound edges.
```
